title: Unbounded task-to-UI channel prevents backpressure from blocking the async background task
description: The AppEvent channel (task → UI) uses tokio::sync::mpsc::unbounded_channel. The rationale is that send() in an async context on a bounded channel would await when the channel is full, blocking the background task while the egui paint loop drains. egui's update() runs on the OS UI thread at the display refresh rate; any hiccup (slow repaint, GC pause, window resize) could cause the channel to fill and stall JMAP I/O — including SSE event processing. Events are small structs (Vec<Chat>, Vec<Message>) and the server-side rate is bounded by the SSE stream, so unbounded memory growth is not a practical risk. The UI-to-task channel (AppCommand) uses std::sync::mpsc::sync_channel(256) in the opposite direction because the UI send() is synchronous (egui update() is not async) and 256 commands is a safe cap for human-speed interaction. A future contributor might suggest switching AppEvent to bounded to add backpressure — that would be the wrong trade-off because the risk (stalled JMAP I/O) outweighs the benefit (bounded memory) given the low event rate.
close_reason: Good decision already implemented in current codebase
